task_completion.description: The agent accessed the CAMEO Chemicals website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open to https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/ or shows search results within the CAMEO Chemicals database.

task_completion.description: The agent created a file with the correct headers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or spreadsheet application shows the headers: 'Chemical Name', 'Flash Point (F)', 'Boiling Point (F)', and 'NFPA 30 Class'.

task_completion.description: The agent entered the correct Flash Point and Boiling Point values for the six chemicals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The document contains the six chemicals with their numeric temperature values in Fahrenheit. Values should be within 10 degrees of the ground truth (e.g., Diethyl Ether FP ~-49, BP ~94; Toluene FP ~40, BP ~231; o-Xylene FP ~90, BP ~291; Acetic Acid FP ~102-104, BP ~244; Benzaldehyde FP ~148, BP ~354; Glycerine FP ~320, BP ~554).

task_completion.description: The agent correctly classified each chemical according to NFPA 30 criteria.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'NFPA 30 Class' column shows: Diethyl Ether as Class IA, Toluene as Class IB, o-Xylene as Class IC, Acetic Acid as Class II, Benzaldehyde as Class IIIA, and Glycerine as Class IIIB.

task_completion.description: The file is saved to the correct path with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or save dialog shows the file named 'flammable_liquid_classes.csv' located in the '~/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to look up the chemicals.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser interacting with the CAMEO Chemicals website, rather than a terminal script fetching the data.

integrity.description: The agent used a text editor or spreadsheet tool to compile the CSV.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a GUI application (like LibreOffice Calc, Gedit, VS Code, etc.) being used to construct the CSV, rather than just a single echo/cat command in the terminal.

integrity.description: The agent did not run a pre-written script containing the answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of a script containing the hardcoded answers being executed in the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
Ground truth values (allow +/- 10°F tolerance):
- Diethyl Ether: FP -49°F, BP 94.3°F -> Class IA
- Toluene: FP 40°F, BP 231.1°F -> Class IB
- o-Xylene: FP 90°F, BP 289-293°F -> Class IC
- Acetic Acid: FP 102-104°F, BP 244°F -> Class II
- Benzaldehyde: FP 148°F, BP 354°F -> Class IIIA
- Glycerine: FP 320°F, BP 554°F -> Class IIIB
Headers must be exactly: 'Chemical Name', 'Flash Point (F)', 'Boiling Point (F)', 'NFPA 30 Class'.
File must be saved at '~/Documents/flammable_liquid_classes.csv'.
